description: LogMein Central is very smooth and even when accessing a remote machine it does not lag.
author: David M.
pros: Log me in is simple to use and deploy, it provides secure access to client computers and makes it easy to share files over different networks and offers a free trial.
cons: So far apart from the price compared to other softwares i have no negative comments on LogMein Central as a software.

review_title: LogMeIn Central allows users to safely access their Business workstations
description: Our experience with LogMeIn Central has been quite good. There is really no learning curve for the user as we can set them up with a simplified login "experience" which directs them to their work desktop.
author: Glen G.
pros: LogMeIn Central allows our users to access their computers without the use of VPNs. Users can access their workstation using their computer, Tablet of phone from the field. Its inexpensive compared to VPNs and allows us to monitor the Employees access.
cons: There are not many issues we have found administering LogMeIn Central. Users don't like the web interface as much on a remote desktop computer, so we push them to download the desktop App to access their workstations.

review_title: An average service for remote access
description: Our overall experience with LogMeIn Central has been okay, we're able to push the content we need and remote into things, but the UI and the connection instability make it very difficult to use.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The user interface, for the application, is easy to use and very plain (no real chance of getting things messed up). It connects easy enough.
cons: The website user interface is very messy, clunky and hard to decipher, making tasks sometimes very difficult to run on remote PC's. Their customer service is very difficult to work with, they are a bit curt and can come off as rude. The connections are not always stable, and can drop unless people have a pristine connection, which is very difficult to monitor.

author: Diana G.
pros: It is a lifesaver when needing to remote into my dental clinic’s management software. Especially since I live over 1 hour away from the office. It is simple to log into, compared to some other services I’ve used in the past. It’s also extremely reliable!
cons: I have not experienced any issues with the program. It’s a lot easier than the Cisco program our office used in the past.

review_title: Log Me In Central A Great Choice For Remote Support
description: I have been using the Central Module for years it allows you to put computers in groups to easily identify your customers computers. Also alerts are set to notify you of Windows updates and antivirus software that is outdated on each computer.
author: Darell B.
pros: Log Me In Central has been my go to remote repair tool for my customers. The software is secure easy to use and is loaded with features.
cons: The pricing a little high to add features it costs to use but every feature is very useful for your business

review_title: Great program but way to expensive
description: We started with LogMeIn when it was a free service and purchased a volume license since we had about 70 computers to use LogMeIn for tech support. However as we grew to over 120 computers the annual license fee grew much faster to were it no longer was worth the cost.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The program is very easy to setup and use.
cons: The expense of the license fee drove us away and to find comparable software for less than 1/20 the cost.

description: We have had nothing but positive results with Logmein so far and its been a few years. Great for doing software updates and easy access to remote PC's and the reliability is something we have come to expect.
author: John W.
pros: The ability to group different departments and offices. It makes it so easy to see what we have in use and which one may have a problem.
cons: Not much negative to say about Logmein. it is very reliable. It is a little bit involved to add a new pc to the system but its not a problem.

review_title: Good overall, great at its niche
description: Overall our users' experiences are positive and we find LMI to be the easiest and most affordable option for helping our users stay connected, and is scalable and manageable for our different clients to all be under one umbrella.
author: Andrew W.
pros: My favorite thing about LMI is the ability to lend devices we have set up in LMI to our clients and still maintain management for them. It's easy for our clients to remote into their work DTs when they are working from home and I can monitor to ensure everything goes well.
cons: When actually remoting in to a selected device, the concept of computer access codes or the choosing to log into the domain of a computer vs a local account can confuse some of our users, we have to include additional specific instruction to help them login.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Our overall experience was good and easy to work through. The billing was problematic.
author: Christie G.
pros: Logmein provided ease of access to our customers that further allowed for support coupled with our employees when needed.
cons: I like least was the cost of the services provided by logmein and the billing through go to assist.

description: It has been a positive experience, very useful for to remote in clients machines for those that do not have a support contract with us. I am able to remote in and assist. The whole interface is user friendly for both sides.
author: Wasek T.
pros: LogMeIn is so handy for me to use on clients that we work on Ad Hoc basis. It is really easy to assist a user to allow remote access. It has many tools such as file transfer, chat box, multiple display selector and works with mobile devices too.
cons: While it gets the job done, it can take time to remote into a users machine as there are multiple steps to follow. User has to download software, enter given code, and multiple pop ups to give permission before remote control can begin.

review_title: The programme is really simple to use and provides exceptional security
description: We now have considerably greater insight into our geographically scattered workstations. It is simple for me to give assignments to all of my team members, and I can now keep track of their progress and create reports on time. A plethora of pre-programmed tasks makes the gadget more practicable and productive.
author: Parth K.
pros: The most significant aspect is how simple and convenient it is to access and accomplish chores at any time. Instead of manually inspecting it on the computer, its UI and information that is provided inside its choices, resources, and other computer information are already available on my end. We increased our LogMeIn footprint to allow our users to work from anywhere and our IT support to assist them from anywhere. Because the tool is simple to use, you may use the basic functionality without having to go through extensive training.
cons: There are several tools in Central that need you to pay an additional fee to use. Instability, occasionally the connections go down and it doesn't reconnect in certain circumstances, but there are also cases when the tool isn't working at all, most likely due to a flaw in the update. During busy times, I had some lag and had to restart the programme.

review_title: Great desktop management tool with a few caveats
description: I was able to wrangle in all of my end-users into one unified platform to both maintain windows and application updates and push out anti-virus installations.
author: Jorge A.
pros: What I like about LMI Central, in the premiere package, is the bundling of Anti-Virus and now application management and updates. The ease of being able to remotely access an end-user's workstation is what totally sells me on the software. In the past, I had to have VPN access to my work network or with other tools, I could only use Windows to access end-users over the web but with LMI, its platform agnostic and they have a great mobile app for iOS and Android that allows you to connect to anyone in your list from virtually any device.
cons: The biggest drawback in my use is the ability to push Windows updates, Anti-Virus installs and application updates. There is a fairly intuitive panel to push out all the requests but using it is like trying to crochet with space gloves, as you never get a real sense of feedback that the changes you requested are actually being implemented in near-real time. Sometimes the end-users workstations falsely report that they are missing installations that I know they are not, so there is a lot of fiddling with the end-users workstation in order to get it to report to LMI correctly. Also, when you've subscribed to the package that includes anti-virus installs, there is no way to centrally manage the AV, as its provided by a third party.

description: Only one business problems have resolved using LogMein and that Is just using the software to get into the remote server of my client and assist in any issues they are having.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: LogMein is a great vpn connect tool to use to quickly connect to a remote server and secure. LogMein web based application is great, functions and icons for the VPN tool is easy to read.
cons: LogMein is proven to be a little challenging for me to use in the last 1 year or so; basically upon going to the web-based application for LogMein initial log in is great but then it will force me to download the LogMein. application directly on my PC, I do not have an option to utilize LogMein from the browser itself; after the download process, i then have to close the browser and start using the App on the PC, which is great but I wish developers for LogMein have a way to notify users about it.

review_title: A fantastic product that allows quick set up and fast remote access to computers and servers.
description: I can't imagine doing my job without this software. Things would be so much more clunky and take twice as long without it. It's that good and that important for offering remote support.
author: Tyler L.
pros: It's easy to use even with hundreds of computers set up, all organized into group folders with a wealth of information available to you before you even remote connect to the user's computer. It has a huge array of customization options and features to access without ever feeling overwhelming, and has support information and access right from the main page. It's extremely useful for remote management, in addition to just being impressive to clients.
cons: They are, bar none, the best remote access and support software on the market. This however means that when they raise their prices, which they seem to do every year, you're left to either take it and raise your own prices slightly, or eat the loss. They also seem to have dropped the live chat support feature, as it's always marked "Offline" on the support drop-down even though it's within the posted hours.

description: Working in the commercial heating and air conditioning industry, it's vital to be able to connect to customer sites to troubleshoot issues when the arise. A lot of times, a fix can be as easy as changing a set point which could take hours to drive to do. This cuts the time to minutes.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: This is an easy option for getting remote access into customer computers. Makes my job a whole lot easier.
cons: The only thing I can think of is I wish it were a bit cheaper. My company doesn't use or need all of the features. We only need remote access to a lot of computers

review_title: This has provided an enterprise solution for less than the expected cost
description: Overall experience has been extremely positive, after the initial setup has been completed LogMeIn scales to any size organisation via GPO installation and I have only very rarely run into major issues with it. I intend to continue using LogMeIn for the foreseeable future.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I am able to set up groups within LogMeIn to specify domain/non-domain/desktop/laptop/server/etc. This makes finding the correct computer much easier LogMeIn can be installed via GPO and when configured correctly functions with zero intervention from IT staff and runs silently in the background so the users are not disturbed by it. Two factor authentication ensures that the account cannot be breached by any means Passwords can be set for each computer so in the extremely unlikely event of 2FA being breached the computers remain secure.
cons: There is a "Trust this computer" or "remember me" button on the login - this does not work if 2FA has been set up. It is confusing and frustrating to keep this button available on the "enter the code we just text you" page when it serves no purpose. On rare occasions LogMeIn has crashed requiring the client to reboot their PC before it becomes accessible again.

review_title: Seamless remote access to all workstations eliminating otherwise time consuming visits in person for simple support tasks.
description: I have used many remote software applications and LogMeIn is hands down the most useful and versatile player in the market. There is nothing I have found the program incapable of accomplishing and I am constantly learning new features that save me time and my clients money. It would take hours to relay all of the benefits of LogMeIn for the real world IT professional.
author: Rhett A.
pros: Instant access to multiple networks through one account interface. Easy setup and deployment of software packages and scripts for multiple scheduled maintenance tasks. File transfers are simply drag and drop convenient. With a wide range of collected data at disposal, workstation health is displayed for all aspects of computer function. There is an option to remotely power on and off computers and manage services without the need to remotely take over the computer.
cons: The only change I would like to see is to eliminate the disconnect that happens momentarily while updating the LogMeIn client software. (It automatically reconnects without intervention.)

review_title: Very helpful product when doing IT support.
description: Changed the way our department is run when doing IT support.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: This software helps us to track all our organization pc's in one place. The dashboard allows you to see the health, updates and antivirus statuses of the organization.
cons: Not inexpensive. When using this product in a large organization the cost can be quite high in order to track all of your assets.

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Agent based remote support - installation of agent is simple and connecting and making use of the product is simple to use - a life saver when needing to access remote servers/computers
cons: A security risk without patching the agent and software often. Support is poor depending on the person who is helping with your issues.

description: LMI Central has saved my team a ton of time but leaves us wanting more features
author: Jon A.
pros: Allows my team to quickly connect to machines across our company. Connections are always fast and reliable.
cons: Severely lacking in features. Alternative solutions like screenconnect are cheaper, just as reliable, and support things like silent command execution, screen monitoring, etc without connecting to the machine. This allows for repair or maintenance without the need to disrupt the end user.

review_title: It's alright but could be better
description: LogMeIn Central does the job but I still had to look for an alternative.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The software does remote desktop well; the resolution is good and even some of the remote management for Windows computer is beneficial.
cons: There is poor support for Mac. The software for Mac doesn't support chat. The layout of the targets in the portal could be better; deployment is not the greatest; push preferences are for higher tier pricing.

description: It's a great service, and was easy to learn the ins and outs.
author: Michael D.
pros: I keep this on all my PCs & laptops. Great solution for keeping secure logins in one place. Very easy to use.
cons: Depending on which version of windows you are on, sometimes it requires you reinstall a couple times to work properly.

author: Matt E.
pros: Easy endpoint and support management. The UI is clean and intuitive. Makes remote support a snap for the help desk.
cons: The cost is way too high. You can find other remote support tools for a lot less and either the same or more features.

description: This program is easy to use and can accomplish what most folks would want to use it for so I would recommend it for routine use.
author: Tammy G.
pros: This was easy to use and set up for 2 computers to access our server. I like the security of the access that it provides and the ability to define access of the user.
cons: When trying to track employee work time I found it difficult with the information provided to determine if someone was actually accessing programs or just logged in. I need more details of tracking employee access than this was able to provide but we continue to use it for employees to work from home and remote in to our server.

description: We have around 480 client desktops on LMI. Some servers, most desktops. We do like their are a local business (we are in the Boston area) and that they are easy to reach. It has allowed us to do a lot more remote support than making physical trips. Travel time and sending a tech can be expensive.
author: Paul R.
pros: We use mostly the free edition of license for our customers within Central, we use other RMMs to obtain Pro if need be (remote printing, file sharing, etc...) It is easy to give remote access to employees of our customers Adding remote users is simple and easy I don't have to keep track of passwords, if a customer forgets theirs, they simply perform the forgot my password routine The interface is simple and there is not much to learn
cons: Somewhat limited with free licenses (remote printing, file sharing) That's about the only issues I have with it.
